records.ety: [L. imbuere; pref. im- in + perh. a disused simple word akin to L. bibere to drink. Cf. Imbibe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imbued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Imbuing.]

defs: To tinge deeply; to dye; to cause to absorb; as, clothes thoroughly imbued with black.
To tincture deeply; to cause to become impressed or penetrated; as, to imbue the minds of youth with good principles.
quotes: Thy words with grace divine Imbued, bring to their sweetness no satiety.
